name: DeFAb
description: DeFAb (Defeasible Abduction Benchmark) is a dataset and generation pipeline that converts legacy knowledge bases into defeasible theories and generates evaluation instances with polynomial-time verifiable gold-standard hypotheses. The dataset comprises 372,402 instances across 33.7 million materialized rules from 15 knowledge sources spanning biology, law, materials science, chemistry, commonsense reasoning, and biomedical informatics. Instances are stratified into three levels: fact completion, rule abduction, and defeater abduction with formal conservativity guarantees.

sc:publisher.sc:name: University of Colorado Boulder, Department of Computer Science

rai:dataCollection: Instances are generated by a deterministic polynomial-time pipeline from publicly available knowledge bases. No crowdsourcing or human annotation is involved except for a small set (35) of high-novelty Level 3 defeaters authored by the paper's author. Source knowledge bases are converted to definite logic programs, then to defeasible theories via a parameterized partition function. Cross-ontology extraction normalizes concept names across sources using lowercased, underscore-separated identifiers.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: Source knowledge bases include: YAGO 4.5 (CC-BY 4.0), WordNet 3.0 (Princeton License), LKIF Core (Apache 2.0), MatOnto (CC-BY 4.0), Wikidata (CC0 1.0), ConceptNet 5.8 (CC-BY-SA 4.0), Gene Ontology (CC-BY 4.0), UMLS 2025AB (NLM License, free for research), SUMO (GPLv2), FrameNet (CC-BY 3.0), MeSH (Public domain, NLM), OpenCyc, BabelNet, and VisualSem. All are publicly available under open licenses.
rai:dataCollectionTimeframe: Source knowledge bases span 1984 (Cyc) to 2025 (UMLS 2025AB, YAGO 4.5).
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: Non-English content is filtered at the extraction stage. ConceptNet NotCapableOf edges are sparse (329 total English edges), limiting automated Level 3 defeater generation from that source.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Source knowledge bases are converted to definite logic programs, then to defeasible theories via a parameterized partition function kappa.
Cross-ontology extraction normalizes concept names across sources using lowercased, underscore-separated identifiers.
Non-English content is filtered at the extraction stage.

Synthetic contamination-control instances use invented predicate names generated by a context-free grammar over phonotactically valid syllable templates, verified for zero occurrence in Common Crawl via the infini-gram index.
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluating foundation models on non-monotonic reasoning, belief revision, and grounded hypothesis generation.
The polynomial-time verifier-backed scoring function makes the dataset suitable as a training signal for preference optimization (DPO, RLHF) without reward model approximation.
Contamination-resistant evaluation of formal reasoning versus memorized associations.
Multimodal (vision-language) evaluation of defeasible reasoning via the M5 modality.
rai:dataLimitations: The base evaluation uses 409 expert-curated Tier 0 instances (35 Level 3); Wilson 95% CI half-widths are +/-15-17 pp on aggregate Level 3 accuracy.
The M1 (narrative) decoder operates approximately by design.
Automated Level 3 generation from Tier 1 remains limited by sparse ConceptNet NotCapableOf edges.
The synthetic contamination control mitigates but does not eliminate the confound: structural patterns of defeasible theories may themselves be memorized.
Defeasible rules encode general defaults and exceptions from knowledge bases, not clinical or legal guidance. Not intended for real-world scientific, legal, or medical decisions.
rai:dataBiases: Knowledge bases reflect the biases and coverage gaps of their creators and funding sources. Tier 0 domains (biology, legal reasoning, materials science) reflect expertise availability rather than representative sampling.
ConceptNet behavioral properties skew toward English-language commonsense knowledge.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Positive: improved evaluation of reasoning deficits may accelerate development of more reliable AI systems. Negative: low risk; formal logic benchmark with no direct path to harmful deployment. The dataset evaluates reasoning capabilities rather than producing actionable predictions.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. No personal data, human subjects, or crowdsourcing involved. All instances are derived from formal logic programs over scientific and commonsense knowledge.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: The dataset will be maintained for at least 5 years from publication by Patrick Cooper at the University of Colorado Boulder. The generation pipeline enables re-generation from updated source KBs. Versioned releases follow semantic versioning (v1.0, v1.1, etc.). Tier 0 evaluation instances are frozen at v1.0 for cross-study comparability; additional tiers are additive. Bug reports and error corrections are tracked via the GitHub repository.

distribution.name: tier0-instances
distribution.description: Tier 0 expert-curated evaluation instances (409 JSON) across biology, legal reasoning, and materials science

distribution.name: tier1-instances
distribution.description: Tier 1 cross-ontology instances (324K) across five domains from OpenCyc + ConceptNet

distribution.name: tier2-instances
distribution.description: Tier 2 domain-specific instances from Gene Ontology, UMLS, SUMO, FrameNet, Wikidata, BabelNet

distribution.name: tier3-instances
distribution.description: Tier 3 encyclopedic instances from YAGO 4.5 full

distribution.name: synthetic-instances
distribution.description: Synthetic contamination-control instances (409) with invented predicate names, structurally matched to Tier 0

recordSet.name: evaluation-instances
recordSet.description: Defeasible abduction evaluation instances with challenge theory, target literal, candidates, and gold-standard hypotheses

field.name: theory
field.description: The challenge defeasible theory (facts, strict rules, defeasible rules)

field.name: target
field.description: The target literal q to be explained

field.name: candidates
field.description: The candidate hypothesis set including distractors

field.name: level
field.description: Instance difficulty level: 1 (fact completion), 2 (rule abduction), or 3 (defeater abduction)

field.name: renderings
field.description: Instance rendered in modalities M1 (narrative), M2 (semi-formal), M3 (annotated formal), M4 (pure formal)
